---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Dependency injection requires injecting three interfaces into the App struct TaskStore/BookmarkStore (Load/Save/Exists), Clock (Now() time.Time), and io.Writer for output. None of these may be called directly in core business logic — only through the injected interfaces. Wire real implementations (FileStore, RealClock, os.Stdout) only in main(). The App struct is constructed via a NewApp() constructor accepting all three. This enables FakeClock and MemStore in tests with no filesystem or wall-clock dependencies..



**Accumulated (bookmarks):** Accumulated from bookmarks build: a FOURTH dependency class beyond Store/Clock/Writer — side-effect FUNCTIONS. Inject e.g. openURL func(string) error as a plain func field (not a named interface), passed to NewApp; override directly in tests (app.openURL = stub) with a package-level no-op var noOpen = func(url string) error { return nil }. Real impl dispatches on runtime.GOOS (open/rundll32/xdg-open) and uses .Start() not .Run() to avoid blocking.
